mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the requirement for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Cluster API components.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This command adds the Cluster API Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for the Cluster API to define its custom resource types. This step installs them into your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.5/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API controller
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API controller using Helm, which manages the lifecycle of Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.5

```

> This command deploys the Cluster API controller into the kube-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource that defines a new Kubernetes cluster to be managed by the Cluster API.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

```

> This command creates a new Cluster resource named 'my-cluster'.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly in your cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> This command lists all pods in the kube-system namespace to verify that the Cluster API controller is running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Cluster API controller
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API controller and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss of managed clusters.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.5/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This command uninstalls the Cluster API controller.
> This command removes the CRDs associated with Cluster API.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Cluster API installation
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster resources. This step includes draining nodes if necessary, upgrading the controller, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.5

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> This command drains all nodes to prepare for the upgrade.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API controller to the specified version.
> This command verifies that the upgraded Cluster API controller is running.
commands: kubectl get nodes -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to install CRDs'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if there are existing CRDs with the same name or version.

**Fix:**
Check for existing CRDs and delete them before reapplying: kubectl delete crd <crd-name>

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is unreachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig and network connectivity to the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in kube-system are in 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the Cluster API controller is misconfigured or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods for errors: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system

troubleshooting.title: Custom resource not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the custom resource definition was not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.5/cluster-api-components.yaml

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved error handling in the controller.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API versions for infrastructure providers.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your custom resources to the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the Cluster API controller running in the kube-system namespace with all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
